Digital technology has opened new possibilities for creating and sharing this music.

documents.title: Musical Instrument: flute
documents.content: The flute is a wind instrument that produces sound through breath creating vibrations in reeds or metal. The instrument plays important roles in cultural and religious traditions.

documents.title: Multiplication Problem: 3 × 5
documents.content: To multiply 3 × 5, think of it as adding 3 to itself 5 times. 3 × 5 = 15. Multiplication is repeated addition.

documents.title: Subtraction: 89 - 44
documents.content: To subtract 44 from 89, we find the difference. 89 - 44 = 45. Subtraction tells us how much is left or the difference between two numbers.

documents.title: Subtraction: 71 - 9
documents.content: To subtract 9 from 71, we find the difference. 71 - 9 = 62. Subtraction tells us how much is left or the difference between two numbers.

documents.title: Division: 140 ÷ 7
documents.content: When we divide 140 ÷ 7, we're asking 'how many 7s fit into 140?' The answer is 20. Division is the inverse of multiplication.

documents.title: Percentage Calculation: 10% of 90
documents.content: To find 10% of 90, multiply by 0.1: 90 × 0.1 = 9.0. Percentages are fractions out of 100.

documents.title: Percentage Calculation: 75% of 120
documents.content: To find 75% of 120, multiply by 0.75: 120 × 0.75 = 90.0. Percentages are fractions out of 100.

documents.title: Division: 176 ÷ 2
documents.content: When we divide 176 ÷ 2, we're asking 'how many 2s fit into 176?' The answer is 88. Division is the inverse of multiplication.

documents.title: Subtraction: 72 - 31
documents.content: To subtract 31 from 72, we find the difference. 72 - 31 = 41. Subtraction tells us how much is left or the difference between two numbers.

documents.title: Basic Addition: 2 + 80
documents.content: To calculate 2 + 80, we add the numbers together. 2 + 80 = 82. Addition is one of the fundamental arithmetic operations.

documents.title: Basic Addition: 82 + 19
documents.content: To calculate 82 + 19, we add the numbers together. 82 + 19 = 101. Addition is one of the fundamental arithmetic operations.

documents.title: Multiplication Problem: 14 × 6
documents.content: To multiply 14 × 6, think of it as adding 14 to itself 6 times. 14 × 6 = 84. Multiplication is repeated addition.

documents.title: Basic Addition: 53 + 21
documents.content: To calculate 53 + 21, we add the numbers together. 53 + 21 = 74. Addition is one of the fundamental arithmetic operations.

documents.title: Percentage Calculation: 25% of 175
documents.content: To find 25% of 175, multiply by 0.25: 175 × 0.25 = 43.75. Percentages are fractions out of 100.

documents.title: Subtraction: 85 - 21
documents.content: To subtract 21 from 85, we find the difference. 85 - 21 = 64. Subtraction tells us how much is left or the difference between two numbers.

documents.title: Division: 18 ÷ 9
documents.content: When we divide 18 ÷ 9, we're asking 'how many 9s fit into 18?' The answer is 2. Division is the inverse of multiplication.

documents.title: Basic Addition: 93 + 6
documents.content: To calculate 93 + 6, we add the numbers together. 93 + 6 = 99. Addition is one of the fundamental arithmetic operations.

documents.title: Percentage Calculation: 90% of 60
